Journal.Title: Fluids and barriers of the CNS

Article.ArticleTitle: Choroid plexus modulates subventricular zone adult neurogenesis and olfaction through secretion of small extracellular vesicles.

Abstract.AbstractText: The choroid plexus (CP) in the brain ventricles secretes cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) that bathes the adjacent subventricular zone (SVZ). As the largest adult neurogenic region enriched with neural stem/progenitor cells (NSPCs), the SVZ supplies newborn neurons to the olfactory bulb (OB) for normal olfaction. This report depicts the presence of a CP-SVZ regulatory (CSR) axis, in which the CP regulates SVZ adult neurogenesis and olfaction via secretion of small extracellular vesicles (sEVs). The proposed CSR axis was supported by the evidence of (1) a direct effect of CP epithelial cells on the SVZ by in vivo transplantation and in vitro CP-SVZ co-culture assays, (2) differential OB neurogenesis following intracerebroventricular (ICV) infusion of sEVs derived from the CP of control or manganese (Mn)-poisoned mice, (3) progressively diminished SVZ adult neurogenesis after CP-selective inhibition of sEV secretion via AAV5-mediated SMPD3 knockdown, and (4) compromised olfactory performance following CP-selective SMPD3-knockdown. Collectively, our findings demonstrate the physiological, toxicological, and behavioral importance of this sEV-dependent CSR axis in the adult brain.
The online version contains supplementary material available at 10.1186/s12987-026-00793-7.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: School of Health Sciences, Purdue University, 550 Stadium Mall Drive, West Lafayette, IN, HAMP-1273, USA.
School of Health Sciences, Purdue University, 550 Stadium Mall Drive, West Lafayette, IN, HAMP-1273, USA.
School of Health Sciences, Purdue University, 550 Stadium Mall Drive, West Lafayette, IN, HAMP-1273, USA. wzheng@purdue.edu.

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: 1. Transplantation and co-culture assays demonstrate direct regulation of CP on the SVZ. 2. sEVs constitute a critical CP secretome fraction that underlies the CSR axis. 3. CP-selective suppression of sEV secretion decreases SVZ neurogenesis and impairs olfaction.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Ethics declaration: Not applicable. Ethics approval of animal experiments: This study was conducted in compliance with standard animal use practices and approved by the Animal Care and Use Committee of Purdue University (PUCAC No. 1112000526). Purdue University follows federal and state laws, including the Animal Welfare Act and the PHS Policy, along with its own institutional guidelines. The university adheres to the “U.S. Government Principles for the Utilization and Care of Vertebrate Animals Used in Testing, Research, and Training”. Consent for publication: Not applicable. Competing interest: The authors declare no competing interests.
